originalTitle: 'It was either killed or be killed' - ongoing nightmares of an ex-child soldier in Somalia
title: Yusuf's Brave Journey: Finding Peace in Somalia
content:
Hello from Somalia!

Hi friends! I'm your friendly news reporter from Africa. Today, we're going to take a special trip to a beautiful country called Somalia. It's a big country in East Africa, right by the big, sparkling Indian Ocean! Imagine long, sandy beaches where the waves gently splash. It's a warm place with lots of sunshine. Did you know? Somalia has the longest coastline in mainland Africa! That means lots and lots of beautiful beaches and places for fishing boats. The capital city of Somalia is called Mogadishu. It's a very old city, full of history, but also a bustling, busy place today with markets, shops, and many friendly people. Think of a big, lively town with lots of energy! In this lively city of Mogadishu, there lives a kind and strong man named Yusuf Ali. He is 34 years old now. That's a grown-up age, maybe a bit older than your parents or an uncle. Yusuf has a very special story to tell us. It's a story about tough times, but also about amazing strength and finding peace after a storm.

What is a "Child Soldier"?

Sometimes, in different parts of the world, there are very sad things called wars. Wars are when groups of people fight each other, and it can be very dangerous. And sometimes, very, very sadly, children are caught in these fights. Even worse, some children are forced to join these fights. These children are called "child soldiers." Did you know? Every child in the world has special rights! These rights mean that children should always be safe. They have the right to play, the right to go to school, the right to have a loving family, and the right to live without fear. Children should never be made to fight in wars. It’s a very important rule for everyone! When Yusuf was just a little boy, much younger than you are now, he became a child soldier. He was made to do grown-up things that were very, very scary and sad. He shouldn't have been doing those things. This is why his story is so important for us to hear. We want to make sure all children can grow up safe and happy.

Yusuf's Past: Hard Memories

Yusuf remembers many, many things from when he was a child. Some of these memories are not happy ones at all. They are like "dark reminders" for him. Imagine if you remembered a very, very scary dream, and that dream kept coming back to your mind even when you were awake. That's a bit like what Yusuf felt sometimes. He used to have many bad dreams, which we call nightmares, because of the scary things he saw and had to do. He would wake up feeling very scared and sad. It's very, very hard to carry these kinds of heavy feelings inside you every day. Yusuf felt like he was in a very difficult place. He said it was like he had to fight to stay alive, making him feel like it was "either killed or be killed." This meant he felt he had no other choice but to do scary things just to survive each day. Did you know? When someone goes through very, very tough and scary times, like what Yusuf experienced, it can stay with them for a long time. These feelings are normal, but they can be very heavy. It's important to remember that it's always okay to feel sad, scared, or even angry sometimes. Talking about these feelings can help a lot. These difficult and sad memories made it hard for Yusuf to live a peaceful and happy life for many years. He carried a heavy heart and a heavy mind. He often thought about his past and wished he could forget some of the very sad parts. He wanted to feel lighter and happier.

Finding Help and Hope

But Yusuf is a truly strong and brave man. He didn't want to live with these heavy, sad feelings forever. He knew deep down that he needed help to feel better and find his own peace. So, he bravely looked for people who could understand him and help him. Did you know? There are special people, like doctors for your feelings, and kind groups called organizations, who help children and grown-ups who have been through wars. They help them talk about their feelings, learn new ways to cope, and find paths to feel better and build happy new lives. Yusuf found these kind people, and he started talking to them. He told them all about his memories, his fears, and his hopes for the future. And slowly, little by little, like a tiny seed growing into a strong plant, talking helped him feel a bit lighter and much more hopeful. It was like sunshine finally breaking through after a very long, cloudy, and rainy day. He learned that he was not alone in his feelings. He learned new and good ways to deal with his sad and scary memories. He understood that it's okay to feel different things, and that feelings can change. And most wonderfully, he learned that he could build a new, strong, and happy life for himself, even after everything he had been through. This is an incredibly brave and amazing thing to do!

Living in Mogadishu Today

Now, Yusuf lives in his home city of Mogadishu. It's his home, and he loves it. He still sees things that might remind him of the past sometimes. But now, he looks at them differently. He doesn't let them make him as sad or scared as before. He has learned to look forward. He is working hard to have a good life. He wants to be a good neighbor, a good friend, and a good citizen. He wants to help others too, especially children, so they never have to face the same things he did. He understands that everyone, every single person, deserves to feel safe, happy, and loved. Did you know? Mogadishu is a city that is working very hard to rebuild and become even better after many years of difficult times. People there are building new schools for children, new homes, and even new parks where kids can play safely. They are creating a hopeful future for everyone. Yusuf is a special part of this new, hopeful Somalia. He is building his own peace, brick by brick, day by day. He knows that even after very, very hard times, good things can happen, and dreams can come true. He hopes that no child, anywhere in the world, ever has to go through what he did.

Why Yusuf's Story is Important

Yusuf's story teaches us many, many important things. It teaches us that war is very sad and hurts many people, especially innocent children. It teaches us that children should never, ever be made to fight in wars. They should always be protected and allowed to be children. It also teaches us about being incredibly brave. Yusuf was brave to ask for help. He was brave to talk about his deepest feelings, even the scary ones. And he is brave every single day as he lives his life with hope and tries to make the world a better place. Did you know? In Africa, there are many, many people like Yusuf who are working hard every day to build a peaceful future for their countries and for all children. They believe in kindness, in helping each other, and in building strong communities. His story reminds us to be kind to everyone we meet. We don't always know what hard things someone has been through in their life. A little bit of kindness, a smile, or a friendly word can make a very, very big difference in someone's day and in their heart.

We Can All Help!

So, what can we, as children and grown-ups, do to help? We can learn about children's rights and make sure everyone knows them. We can tell our grown-ups that children should always be safe and loved. We can be kind to our friends, our family, and everyone around us. And we can always hope and work for a world where all children can play freely, go to school, and feel happy and safe every single day, without any worries or fears. Yusuf's journey from a difficult past to finding peace is a shining example for us all. It shows us that even after the toughest times, hope can grow, kindness can heal, and peace is always possible. And that, my dear friends, is a wonderful and powerful message for all of us! Let's all work together to make our world a more peaceful and loving place for every child.
summary: Yusuf Ali, a brave man from Somalia, shares his journey of overcoming sad memories from being a child soldier to find peace and hope today.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that all children should be safe, not fight in wars, and that even after very hard times, people can find help, hope, and build a peaceful life. It shows us why kindness and protecting children are so important for every child in the world.

originalTitle: An Eid celebration on a Gaza rooftop turns into a ‘horror movie’
title: A Big Celebration and Wishing for Happy Homes!
content:
Hello, my little friends from all over Africa! Your friendly news reporter is here to talk about something very important: celebrations, homes, and wishing for happiness for everyone. We know how much fun it is to celebrate, right? Let's chat about a special celebration called Eid and how important it is for all children to feel safe and happy in their homes.

What is Eid?

Eid is a very, very special holiday for many people around the world, especially for our Muslim brothers and sisters. Think of it a bit like Christmas or Easter, but with its own unique traditions! On Eid, families get together, dress in beautiful new clothes, and share yummy food. There are often gifts, lots of laughter, and games. Children love Eid because it's a time for joy and togetherness. Did you know? There are actually two main Eid celebrations each year! One is called Eid al-Fitr, which marks the end of a special fasting month, and the other is Eid al-Adha, which is also a time for big feasts and sharing.

Children Love to Celebrate!

Every child, no matter where they live – in a big city in Nigeria, a small village in Kenya, or a bustling town in South Africa – loves to celebrate. We love parties, playing games with our friends, eating special treats, and spending time with our families. These celebrations make us feel happy, loved, and part of something special. They are memories we keep in our hearts forever. Did you know? In many parts of Africa, we have so many different festivals and celebrations throughout the year! From harvest festivals to traditional dance ceremonies, everyone loves to come together and have fun.

A Special Celebration in a Faraway Place

Now, let's imagine a place far, far away from us, where children were also very excited for Eid. In a special town there, many families live close together. On Eid, these children were looking forward to celebrating on their rooftops! Rooftops can be wonderful places in warm countries. You can see the stars, feel the cool evening air, and sometimes even have a little party up there. The children were probably dreaming of balloons, special lanterns, and running around with their cousins. Did you know? In some places, especially where homes are very close together, rooftops become extra outdoor spaces for drying clothes, growing plants, or even having family gatherings!

Sometimes Things Are Not Easy

But sometimes, even when we are planning to be very happy, unexpected things can happen that make celebrations difficult. Imagine you are all ready for a big party, wearing your best clothes, and then something makes everyone feel sad or scared. Maybe a big noise scares everyone, or grown-ups start looking very worried. Instead of playing and laughing, children might feel confused or unsafe. It’s a very sad feeling when your home, the place where you should feel safest, suddenly feels scary. Did you know? Everyone, even grown-ups, feels sad or scared sometimes. It's okay to have these feelings, and it's important to talk about them with someone you trust.

Wishing for Safe Homes

Every single child in the world deserves to have a safe home. A home is not just a building; it’s a place where you are loved, where you can play, learn, sleep peacefully, and feel protected. When homes are not safe, it makes children very, very sad. It’s hard to imagine not being able to play freely in your own home or feeling worried all the time. Children need safe homes to grow up strong, happy, and ready to learn wonderful things. Did you know? A safe and happy home is one of the most important things for a child's brain and body to grow healthy and strong!

How We Can Help

Even though we are far away, we can still do something important. We can send our kindest thoughts and wishes for peace and safety to all children who are going through tough times. We can also remember to be extra kind to the people around us in our own communities. Sharing a toy, helping a friend, or giving a big hug can make a huge difference. Every little act of kindness makes the world a better place, and it reminds us that we are all connected. Did you know? A simple smile or a kind word can make someone's day much brighter, and it doesn't cost anything at all!

The Power of Hope!

Even when things are difficult and sad, hope is a very powerful thing. Hope is like a little light inside us that helps us believe that good things will happen again. It helps us wish for brighter days, for peace, and for all children to be able to celebrate happily in their safe homes. We must always hold onto hope and wish for a world where every child can laugh, play, and celebrate without any worries. Did you know? Hope can give us strength even when we feel tired or sad, helping us to look forward to the future! Let’s all keep wishing for a world where children everywhere can enjoy every celebration to the fullest, always feeling safe and sound in their homes. Thank you for listening, my little friends!
summary: This news item talks about Eid celebrations and how all children deserve to feel safe and happy in their homes, even when things are difficult in faraway places.
whyMatters: This is important because every child deserves a safe place to live and to celebrate happy moments with their family, and we should always wish for peace for everyone.

originalTitle: This was a training game - England & Tuchel now need to get serious
title: Big Football Fun: Getting Ready for the World Cup!
content:
Hello, Football Friends!

Hello, hello, my amazing friends! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from the world of football! Can you hear the crowd cheer? Can you see the players run? Today, we are talking about a big game and how a team gets ready to play its very best. It’s like getting ready for the biggest school play or a super important drawing competition! We want to learn about practice!

What is Football?

First, let's talk about football! What is this amazing game? Football is a sport where two teams play together on a big green field. Each team has eleven players. They use their feet to kick a round ball. Their goal is to get the ball into the other team's net. This net is called a goal! If you score more goals than the other team, your team wins! It makes everyone jump with joy! *Did you know?* A football is not perfectly round when it's flat! It gets its perfect round shape when it's filled with air. It's usually made from leather or special strong plastic.

The Super Big World Cup!

Now, imagine the biggest football party in the whole wide world! That is what we call the World Cup! Many, many countries send their best football teams to play in this tournament. They all want to win the shiny golden trophy! It only happens every four years, so it is very, very special. Everyone watches from their homes, cheering for their favorite teams. It's a time when everyone comes together for football fun! *Did you know?* The first-ever World Cup was played way back in 1930! That's a very long time ago, even before your parents were born!

Meet Team England

Today, we are talking about a team called England. They are a football team from a country far, far away, across the big ocean. Team England is one of the teams that will play in the World Cup! They have amazing players who can kick the ball very well and run super fast. They want to show everyone how good they are and try to win the World Cup for their country.

Practice Time is Very Important!

Before you do something big, like a school exam or a dance show, what do you do? You practice, practice, practice! Right? It's the same for football teams! Practicing helps players learn how to play better. It helps them work together like one big team. They learn to pass the ball to each other and score goals. Practice makes them stronger and smarter on the field. *Did you know?* Even the very best football players in the world still practice almost every single day! They know that practice helps them stay amazing.

A Special Practice Game

So, Team England has a very special game coming up. This game is not part of the World Cup yet. It is a "training game" or a "practice game." Think of it like a dress rehearsal for a play. They play against another team, but it's mainly to try out new things and see what works best. It helps them fix any small problems before the really big World Cup games begin. *Did you know?* Sometimes, in practice games, teams try out new players to see if they are ready for the big games!

Learning and Getting Stronger

In this practice game, Team England will try to learn many things. They will practice their passing. They will practice shooting the ball into the goal. They will practice how to defend their own goal so the other team cannot score. Most importantly, they will practice working as a team. Every player needs to know what the other players are doing. It's like all the instruments in a band playing together perfectly. *Did you know?* Teamwork is super important in football! If one player tries to do everything alone, the team usually doesn't win. They need to share the ball and help each other.

Getting Ready to Be Serious!

This practice game is the very last one before the World Cup starts for England. That means they need to use this game very, very wisely! They must learn all they can. They need to play their best so they feel super confident for the real games. After this practice, they will be "serious" and ready to play their absolute hardest to win in the World Cup! *Did you know?* A good coach helps the team learn from their practice games. They watch carefully and tell the players what they did well and what they can do better.

Why We Love Football in Africa

Here in Africa, we love football so much! We cheer for our own amazing teams, and we also love watching teams from all over the world. Football brings us joy, excitement, and sometimes even tears! It teaches us about teamwork, never giving up, and celebrating together. It's a game that brings everyone closer, no matter where you are from.

Looking Forward to the World Cup!

So, let's cheer for all the teams, including Team England, as they get ready for the World Cup! We hope they use their practice game well and play with all their heart. It will be an amazing time filled with incredible goals, fast running, and lots of happy moments. Get ready for the World Cup fun, everyone! We can't wait to watch!
summary: England's football team has one more practice game to get ready for the big World Cup, and they need to use this time to play their very best.
whyMatters: It's important for teams to practice a lot so they can play well and work together, just like practicing for a school play or a big test, to be ready for the exciting World Cup!

originalTitle: Are Portugal better without Ronaldo - and is he undroppable?
title: Our Superstar Ronaldo and Team Portugal!
content:
Hey amazing kids! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here! Today, we're going to talk about a super-duper football player you might know, named Cristiano Ronaldo! He plays for a country called Portugal, and we're going to learn all about him and his team, especially for the big football party called the World Cup!

What is Football?

Football is a super fun game where two teams try to kick a ball into the other team's net. It's like a big race and a big puzzle all at once! You run, you pass, you kick, and you score! Millions of children, just like you, love to play football all around the world. Did you know? Football is the most popular sport on Earth! People call it 'soccer' in some places, but it's the same wonderful game!

What is the World Cup?

Imagine the biggest, most exciting football party in the whole wide world! That's the World Cup! Teams from many, many countries play against each other to see who is the very best football team on the planet. It's like a giant tournament where everyone cheers for their favourite country. It only happens every four years, so it's very special! Did you know? The last World Cup was held in a country called Qatar! It was super exciting!

Meet Our Hero: Cristiano Ronaldo!

Cristiano Ronaldo is one of the most famous football players ever! He comes from a country far away called Portugal. He is known for scoring amazing goals and working super hard. He runs super fast, he kicks the ball super hard, and he loves to win! He's like a superhero on the football field. Did you know? Ronaldo has scored more goals than almost anyone else in football history! He is truly a goal-scoring machine!

Ronaldo and His Team: Portugal!

Cristiano Ronaldo plays for his country's team, Portugal. When he puts on his Portugal jersey, he plays with his friends from his own country. They all want to make their country proud! This World Cup will be his sixth time playing in this huge competition, which is a lot!

Teamwork Makes the Dream Work!

Even though Ronaldo is a superstar, football is a team game! This means everyone on the team has to work together like a big family. The players pass the ball to each other, help each other defend, and celebrate goals together. One player alone cannot win the game; they all need to be a team! Did you know? A football team has 11 players on the field at one time! They all have different jobs, like stopping goals or scoring goals!

Can Portugal Play Well Without Ronaldo All the Time?

Now, here's a big question some grown-ups are asking: Is Portugal a better team *sometimes* when Ronaldo isn't playing every single minute of every game? It sounds strange, right? Because he's so good! Well, imagine this: if Ronaldo is always the one who everyone passes to for scoring, maybe other players don't get as many chances to show their own amazing skills. Portugal has many other super good players now. These players are also very fast, very strong, and can score amazing goals. Sometimes, when Ronaldo is not on the field for a little while, these other players get more chances to kick the ball and try to score. This can make the whole team feel stronger because *everyone* gets a chance to be a hero! It's like having many leaders instead of just one. It makes the team very exciting to watch because goals can come from anywhere!

Is Ronaldo Always Going to Play? (Is He 'Undroppable'?)

Another big word people use is 'undroppable.' It means: can the coach *never* take him out of the game? Even a superstar like Ronaldo might not play every single minute of every single game. Sometimes, a coach wants to try new players, or maybe Ronaldo needs a little rest, or perhaps the team needs a different plan for a specific game. Just like in your school play, sometimes different actors get different roles, right? The coach's job is to choose the best players for each game to help the team win. So, even if Ronaldo is a legend, the coach might sometimes ask him to sit and rest, or let other players play, so the whole team stays super strong and fresh for the World Cup journey! Did you know? A coach is like the captain of a ship, guiding the team and making important decisions!

What's Next for Portugal?

No matter what, Cristiano Ronaldo is a hero, and he will always be a big part of Portugal's team. The World Cup is going to be so exciting! We can't wait to see Portugal play and cheer for them, and for Ronaldo, and for all the amazing teamwork! Let's hope they have a wonderful World Cup adventure! Bye-bye for now, from your friend in Africa! Keep smiling and playing football!
summary: This news talks about superstar Cristiano Ronaldo, his country's team Portugal, and if the team plays well when he's not always on the field for the upcoming World Cup.
whyMatters: It's important because it helps us understand that even the best players are part of a team, and everyone working together is what truly makes a football team strong and exciting to watch, especially for a big event like the World Cup.
